danger_signals.signal: Donkey Kong holding the target number
danger_signals.visual_form: Large ape character sprite at top-center of screen displaying a number
danger_signals.meaning: Active challenge/goal state; represents the mathematical problem to solve

danger_signals.signal: Numbers and operators scattered on vines/chains
danger_signals.visual_form: Digit and arithmetic symbol sprites (+ − × ÷) positioned throughout climbing routes
danger_signals.meaning: Active obstacles/puzzle elements; player must navigate to collect correct combination

safety_signals.visual_form: Vertical plant structures connecting platforms, inherited from Donkey Kong Jr. visual language
safety_signals.meaning: Safe navigation routes; established mechanic for reaching numbers and operators

safety_signals.visual_form: Static platform at left-middle (Player 1) and bottom-right (Player 2) screen locations
safety_signals.meaning: Safe spawn/reset point; visual anchor for player orientation in multiplayer mode

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ape character sprite holding mathematical problem
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Donkey Kong (father) appears only at top-center screen position; no enemy variant documented
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Target number display; occupies dominant screen position indicating control over challenge

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Junior primate character sprite; documented variant includes 'hands up' pose from original game
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Positioned on vines/chains; lower screen positions in two-player layout indicate subordinate position in hierarchical relationship

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Donkey Kong displays target number at screen top; scattered digit and operator symbols appear on vines/chains throughout playfield
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must climb and collect number combinations to reach target; accumulation of apples shown as win counter

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fixed mathematical puzzle displayed with pre-positioned numbers and operators on chains
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player solves predefined equation; singular solution path rather than multiple possible combinations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Junior appears alone on middle-left starting platform; full screen width available for navigation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player controls single character; increased mobility and access to all vine/chain routes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player 1 positioned at middle-left; Player 2 positioned at bottom-right; visual split of screen resources
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Both players race to solve same mathematical problem; spatial separation creates competition dynamic

environmental_storytelling.example: Donkey Kong positioned at top-center screen dominates vertical space; Donkey Kong Jr. starts at lower-middle, visually establishing authority/subordination relationship inherited from Donkey Kong Jr. arcade narrative
Engine, sprites, and vine/chain climbing mechanics directly copied from Donkey Kong Jr.; visual language maintains family conflict framing despite educational content shift
Japanese version uses primitive logo matching sister game Popeye no Eigo Asobi; International version features detailed colorful title screen suggesting different marketing positioning
Remnants of original Donkey Kong Jr. narrative elements visible in unused assets (Mario sprites, caged Donkey Kong, enemies); visual traces of stripped narrative scaffold

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary at screen top; holds number display; sets mathematical problem for Junior to solve
Climbs vines/chains; collects digit and operator symbols; moves toward combining correct numbers to match target
Static positioning on climbing routes; require Junior to navigate toward and collect; visual puzzle piece manifestation
Displays at screen top; remains visible throughout solve attempt; visual anchor showing objective state

notes: Donkey Kong Jr. Math presents minimal world-state communication complexity. The game strips away enemy threats and platforming hazards from the original Donkey Kong Jr., replacing them with mathematical puzzles. Visual communication relies entirely on inherited character positioning and sprite language from the arcade game, with educational content overlaid through number/operator symbols on climbing paths. The father-son relationship established in the original game carries implicit authority structure: Donkey Kong sets challenges, Donkey Kong Jr. solves them. Regional differences in title screen design (primitive Japanese vs. detailed international) suggest different market positioning but do not alter core visual communication of world-state. No faction conflict or political systems exist; gameplay is purely mechanical/educational problem-solving framed within existing character relationship.
